name: Diana Ross
abstract: Diana Ross (American English: ; UK: ; born March 26, 1944) is an American singer and actress. She was the lead singer of the vocal group the Supremes, who became Motown's most successful act during the 1960s and one of the world's best-selling girl groups of all time.

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Schaap
about_quotes.evidence: We played, a trombone player, a tenor player and me, we’d play a chord behind some singer trying to be the next Diana Ross. She wasn’t that famous yet, there was just the Supremes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Schaap interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...Diana Ross. [INTERVIEWEE] No. You know, I think that only -- that's the black race that does that...

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Holman
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with The Association and The Fifth Dimension and Diana Ross, and I got to work with strings.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Holman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Melba Liston
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] What about Diana Ross? How was she? That was at Motown? [INTERVIEWEE] [cannot recall.]
about_quotes.doc_title: Melba Liston — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: I’ll go in and bring the charts and work with Diana Ross to do the charts Billie sang.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I saw the film and Diana Ross did to me one of the greatest bits of acting.
